title: Aminobenzoic acid derivatives for use as anti-inflammatory agents, anti-metastatic agents and/or anticancer agents
abstract: There are provided compounds of formula (IF) in which R 2 and R 11 can represent various different possibilities. These compounds can be useful as anticancer agents as well as anti-inflammatory agents, anti-proliferative agents and/or anti-metastatic agents.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A compound of formula (IF) R 2 is H, or a substituted or unsubstituted member chosen from acetyl, propiolyl, butyryl, isobutyryl and benzoyl; Rn is a substituted or unsubstituted member chosen from phenyl, phenylethenyl, furanyl, thiophenyl, pyridinyl, naphthyl, quinolyl and isoquinolyl; wherein Rn, when substituted, are substituted with at least one substituent chosen from -ORi 2 , -F, -Cl, -Br, -I, acetyl, propiolyl, butyryl, isobutyryl, benzoyl, -NO 2 , -CH 2 OH, nitrophenyl, phenylethenyl, -CF 3 , C 1 -C 8 alkyl, C 2 -C 8 alkenyl, C 2 -C 8 alkynyl, C 3 -C 8 cycloalkenyl, methoxy carbonyl- and alkyloxy carbonyl-; R 12 is H, acetyl, propiolyl, Ci-Cs alkyl, C 2 -C 8 alkenyl, C 2 -C 8 alkynyl or C 3 -C 8 cycloalkenyl; or an enantiomer, diastereoisomer, racemic mixture, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, solvate or prodrug thereof. 2. The compound of claim 1, wherein Rn is a substituted or unsubstituted member chosen from phenyl, furanyl and thiophenyl. 3. A compound of formula (IG) R 2 is H, or a substituted or unsubstituted member chosen from acetyl, propiolyl, butyryl, isobutyryl and benzoyl; R 12 is H, acetyl, propiolyl, Ci-Cs alkyl, C 2 -C 8 alkenyl, C 2 -C 8 alkynyl or C3-C8 cycloalkenyl; R 13, R 14 and R 15 are each independently chosen from H, -OR 12 , -F, -Cl, -Br, -I, acetyl, propiolyl, butyryl, isobutyryl, benzoyl, -NO 2 , Ci-Cs alkyl, C 2 -C8 alkenyl, C 2 -C8 alkynyl, C3-C8 cycloalkenyl, methoxycarbonyl- and alkyloxy carbonyl-; or an enantiomer, diastereoisomer, racemic mixture, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, solvate or prodrug thereof. 4. The compound of claim 3, wherein at least one of R 13, R 14 and R 15 is different from H. 5. The compound of claim 3, wherein at least one of R 13, R 14 and R 15 is -OH. 6. The compound of claim 3, wherein at least one of R 13, Ri 4 and R 15 is -OCH3. 7. The compound of claim 3, wherein at least one of R 13, R H and R 15 is C2-C8 alkyl. 8. The compound of claim 3, wherein at least one of R 13, R 14 and R 15 is -OR 12 in which R 12 is C 2 -C 8 alkenyl. 9. The compound of claim 3, wherein said compound is chosen from 10. The compound of claim 3, wherein said compound is chosen from 11. The compound of claim 1, wherein Rn 2,4-dihydroxyphenyl, 3,5-ditertbutyl-4- 4-methoxyphenyl, hydroxybenzaldehyde, 2-hydroxy -3- 3,4-dimethoxyphenyl, 4-allyloxyphenyl, methoxyphenyl, 2-furanyl, 2-pyridyl, 3-pyridyl, 4-pyridyl, phenylethenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, 4-fluorophenyl, 4-trifluoromethylphenyl and 4-nitrophenyl. 12. A compound of formula (IH) wherein Ri 6 is chosen from 2,4-dihydroxyphenyl, 3,5-ditertbutyl-4- 4-methoxyphenyl, hydroxybenzaldehyde, 

2-hydroxy -3- 3,4-dimethoxyphenyl, 4-allyloxyphenyl, methoxyphenyl, 2-furanyl, 2-pyridyl, 3-pyridyl, 4-pyridyl, phenylethenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, 4-fluorophenyl, 4-trifluoromethylphenyl and 4-nitrophenyl. 13. A compound of formula (IJ) wherein Rn is chosen from 4-hydroxy -3- 3-hydroxy -4- phenyl, methoxyphenyl, methoxyphenyl, 2-hydroxyphenyl, 3 -hydroxyphenyl, 4-hydroxyphenyl, 2,4-dihydroxyphenyl, 3,5-ditertbutyl-4- 4-methoxyphenyl, hy droxybenzaldehy de , 2-hydroxy-3- 3,4-dimethoxyphenyl, 4-allyloxyphenyl, methoxyphenyl, 2-furanyl, 2-pyridyl, 3-pyridyl, 

4-pyridyl, phenylethenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, 4-fluorophenyl, 4-trifluoromethylphenyl, 4-nitrophenyl, 3-nitrophenyl, 2-nitrophenyl, 4-methylphenyl, 3-methylphenyl, 2-methylphenyl, 3 -methoxypheny 1, 2-methoxyphenyl, 4-bromophenyl, 5-methyl -2 -fur anyl, 5-hydroxymethyl-2-furanyl, 5 -nitro-2-furany 1, 5-(4-nitrophenyl)-2-furanyl and 3-furanyl. 14. The compound of claim 13, wherein R17 is chosen from 4-hydroxy-3-methoxyphenyl, 3-hydroxy-4-methoxyphenyl, phenyl, 2-hydroxyphenyl, 3-hydroxyphenyl, 4-hydroxyphenyl, 2,4-dihydroxyphenyl, 3 ,5 -ditertbutyl-4- 4-methoxyphenyl, hy droxybenzaldehy de , 2 -hydroxy -3-methoxyphenyl, 3,4-dimethoxyphenyl, 4-allyloxyphenyl, 2-furanyl, 2-pyridyl, 3-pyridyl, 4-pyridyl, phenylethenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, 4-fluorophenyl, 4-trifluoromethylphenyl and 4-nitrophenyl. 15. The compound of any one of claims 1 to 14, for use in medicine. 16. The compound of any one of claims 1 to 14, for use in reducing risks of developing at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 17. The compound of any one of claims 1 to 14, for use in the treatment of at least one cancer chosen from breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 18. The compound of any one of claims 1 to 14, for use as an anticancer agent. 19. The compound of any one of claims 1 to 14, for use as an anti-inflammatory agent. 20. The compound of any one of claims 1 to 14, for use as an anti-metastatic agent 21. The compound of any one of claims 1 to 14, for use as an anti-proliferative agent. 22. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14, for for treating at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 23. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14, for reducing the risks of developing at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 24. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14, for inhibiting cancer cell growth of at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 25. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14, for inhibiting cancer tumor growth and/or cancer tumor size of at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 26. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14, for for decreasing and/or preventing cancer tumor metastases of at least one cancer 

 chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 27. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 in the manufacture of a medicament for treating at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 28. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 in the manufacture of a medicament for reducing the risks of developing of at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 29. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 in the manufacture of a medicament for inhibiting cancer cell growth of at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 30. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 in the manufacture of a medicament for inhibiting cancer tumor growth and/or cancer tumor size of at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 31. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 in the manufacture of a medicament for decreasing and/or preventing cancer tumor metastases of at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer prostate cancer and bladder cancer. 32. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 in the manufacture of a medicament for inhibiting cancer tumor growth and/or cancer tumor size of at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, lung cancer, colon cancer, uterine/endometrial cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer, and bladder cancer. 33. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 in the manufacture of a medicament for decreasing and/or preventing cancer tumor metastases of at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, lung cancer, colon cancer, uterine/endometrial cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer, and bladder cancer. 34. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 in the manufacture of a medicament for decreasing and/or preventing pathological humoral and cell-mediated inflammation of at least one disease chosen from inflammatory diseases (Crohnâs disease, rheumatoid arthritis, asthma, coeliac disease, glomerulonephritis, hepatitis, inflammatory bowel disease, reperfusion injury or transplant rejection); pregnancy complications (preeclampsia or preterm birth); or gynecological pathologies (endometriosis, uterine leiomyoma, polycystic ovary syndrome or recurrent miscarriage). 35. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 for use as an anticancer agent. 36. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 for use as an anti-inflammatory agent. 37. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 for use as an anti-metastatic agent. 38. Use of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14 for use as an anti-proliferative agent. 39. A method for treating cancer or at least one cancer chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer and bladder cancer, said method comprising administering to a subject in need thereof an effective amount of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14. 40. A method for reducing the risks of developing cancer or for reducing the risk of developing at least one cancer in a subject, the cancer being, for example, chosen from melanoma, breast cancer, uterine cancer, ovarian cancer, prostate cancer and bladder cancer, said method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14. 41. A method for inhibiting cancer cell growth, the method comprising administering to a subject in need thereof an effective amount of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein said cancer cell growth is inhibited by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70% or about 80% relative to an untreated subject. 43. A method for inhibiting cancer cell growth, the method comprising contacting cancer cells with an effective amount of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein a relative STAT1 activation of said cells is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70% or about 80% relative to untreated cancer cells. 45. The method of claim 43 or 44, wherein a relative STAT3 activation of said cells is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70% or about 80% relative to untreated cancer cells. 46. The method of any one of claims 43 to 45 , wherein a CD40 expression of said cells is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70% or about 80% relative to untreated cancer cells. 47. The method of any one of claims 43 to 46, wherein a MHC-II expression of said cells is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70% or about 80% relative to untreated cancer cells. 48. The method of any one of claims 43 to 47, wherein a production of NO of said cells is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70% or about 80% relative to untreated cancer cells. 49. The method of any one of claims 43 to 48, wherein TNFCX/NFKB and/or IL6/STAT3 signaling pathways of said cells is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70% or about 80% relative to untreated cells. 50. The method of any one of claims 43 to 49, wherein an LPS-activated NFKB signaling pathway of said cells is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70% or about 80% relative to untreated cells. 51. A method for inhibiting cancer tumor growth and/or cancer tumor size, the method comprising administering to a subject in need thereof an effective amount of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14. 52. The method of claim 51, wherein the tumor growth and/or tumor size is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70%, about 80% or about 90% relative to an untreated subject. 53. A method for decreasing and/or preventing cancer tumor metastases, the method comprising administering to a subject in need thereof an effective amount of at least one compound as defined in any one of claims 1 to 14. 54. The method of claim 53, wherein a number of tumor metastases is decreased by at least about 10%, about 20%, about 30%, about 40%, about 50%, about 60%, about 70%, about 80% or about 90% relative to an untreated subject.
